gs.header.help.crowdinContext: Label for the help button in the global application header. Clicking this opens the help menu or documentation.

gs.header.help.label.crowdinContext: ARIA label for the help links section in the help menu. Used for accessibility purposes to describe the collection of help-related links.

gs.header.logout.crowdinContext: Label for the logout button in the user menu. Clicking this signs the user out of their current session.

gs.header.menu.crowdinContext: Label for the menu title in vertical mobile menu.

gs.header.accessibility.label.crowdinContext: ARIA label for the main header section of the application. Used by screen readers to identify the header area.

gs.header.href.accessibility.crowdinContext: Accessibility text describing the action of clicking the application logo in the header, which navigates to the homepage.

gs.header.logo.title.accessibility.crowdinContext: Accessibility text for the application logo in the header. Used by screen readers to identify the logo element.

gs.header.mainMenu.ariaLabel.crowdinContext: Accessibility label for the main menu button in the header, used by screen readers

gs.header.menu.accessibility.label.crowdinContext: ARIA label for the primary navigation menu in the header. Used by screen readers to identify the main navigation area.

gs.header.account.title.crowdinContext: Title for the user account menu in the header. Displays user-related options like profile settings and logout.

gs.header.university.crowdinContext: Link to GoodData University training resources

gs.host.error.applicationFailedToLoad.crowdinContext: Heading displayed when a pluggable application module fails to mount or load.

gs.host.error.failedToLoad.crowdinContext: Heading displayed when the application platform context fails to load during startup.

gs.host.error.pageNotFound.crowdinContext: Heading displayed when the user navigates to a URL that does not match any known application or route.

gs.host.error.pageNotFoundDescription.text: The page you are looking for does not exist or you do not have access to it.
gs.host.error.pageNotFoundDescription.crowdinContext: Description shown below the 'Page not found' heading, explaining that the requested page is either missing or inaccessible.

gs.host.error.somethingWentWrong.crowdinContext: Heading displayed when an unexpected error occurs during application redirect resolution.

gs.host.notification.newDeployment.messa.text: A new version of GoodData is available.
gs.host.notification.newDeployment.messa.crowdinContext: Toast message shown when the host detects that a newer build of the application has been deployed while the user's tab was open. Followed by a reload link.

gs.host.notification.newDeployment.reloa.crowdinContext: Clickable text inside the new-deployment toast that triggers a full page reload to load the latest application version.

gs.header.helpMenu.gettingStarted.crowdinContext: Link to getting started guide in help menu

gs.header.helpMenu.connectData.crowdinContext: Link to data connection guide in help menu

gs.header.helpMenu.manage.ws.crowdinContext: Link to workspace management documentation in help menu

gs.header.helpMenu.manage.user.crowdinContext: Link to user management documentation in help menu

messages.genAi.visualisation.saved.succe.crowdinContext: Success message when generative AI visualization is saved

messages.genAi.visualisation.saved.error.text: Oops, there was an issue when saving your visualization.
messages.genAi.visualisation.saved.error.crowdinContext: Error message when generative AI visualization save fails
Detailed error message format. {errorType} and {errorMessage} are replaced with specific error details.

messages.genAi.visualisation.link.copied.text: The visualization link has been copied to your clipboard.

messages.showMore.crowdinContext: Button text to expand and show more message content

messages.showLess.crowdinContext: Button text to collapse and show less message content

gen-ai.ask-assistant.search.crowdinContext: This is used as prefix for the search term when the user starts a new conversation related to a requested term. For example: "I want to show more about: <term>".
